title: Tamil Paruppu with Ghee

subs.name: moong dal (paasi paruppu) for a lighter, more digestible version — reduce pressure to 3 whistles

ingredients.name: ghee (nei)
visual_checks: must be ghee — no substitution for this dish's character
ingredients.notes: must be ghee — no substitution for this dish's character

subs.name: coconut oil for a dairy-free option, though the character changes completely

steps.text: Rinse the toor dal twice until the water runs clear. Pressure cook with 2 cups of water and turmeric for 4 whistles on medium flame. Let the pressure release naturally before opening.
Mash the cooked dal with a ladle until smooth and creamy. Add salt and adjust the consistency with hot water if needed — it should flow slowly off a spoon, not be as thick as paste.
Heat the ghee in a small tempering pan over medium flame. Add cumin seeds and let them sizzle and turn golden, about 20 seconds. Add dried red chillies and asafoetida and fry for 10 seconds more.

Pour the sizzling ghee tempering over the hot mashed dal. Stir gently, scatter the coriander leaves, and serve immediately over steamed rice. The aroma of ghee and cumin rising from a bowl of hot paruppu is among the most comforting in Tamil cooking.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.

substitution_notes: toor-dal → moong dal (paasi paruppu) for a lighter, more digestible version — reduce pressure to 3 whistles; ghee → coconut oil for a dairy-free option, though the character changes completely
